In 2017, reports documented that seven transgender women had been killed in the United States in the early part of the year, raising urgent concerns about violence against transgender people. In response, Democratic lawmakers called on Attorney General Jeff Sessions and the Department of Justice to investigate these cases as hate crimes and to prioritize efforts addressing anti-transgender violence. Advocates argued that such federal action was critical for ensuring justice for victims and for sending a message against discrimination and violence targeting the transgender community.
